title: astragalus coahuilae
intro: Astragalus coahuilae is a perennial legume plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the pea family and is also known as Coahuila milkvetch or Mexican milkvetch. Astragalus coahuilae is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and its health benefits include boosting the immune system, reducing stress and fatigue, and improving cardiovascular health.
